name: Swordfish Escabeche
recipeInstructions:
----------------------------------MARINADE----------------------------------
1/4 cup Lime juice
1 teaspoon Salt
1 1/2 cups Water
-----------------------------------SAUCE-----------------------------------
3/4 teaspoon Oregano, toasted
1 cinnamon stick
2 bay leaves
1 large garlic clove, peeled and minced
1/2 teaspoon sugar
1/3 teaspoon black peppercorns
1/2 teaspoon cumin seeds (toasted)
1 whole clove
----------------------------------ASSEMBLY----------------------------------
3/4 cup Red wine vinegar
1/4 cup Olive oil
2 red chilies, sliced into thin rings
1/2 medium sweet onion, sliced into thin rings
Toast the oregano until it gives off a noticeable aroma; avoid burning.
The oregano will start to smoke lightly when ready.
Mince the garlic and sauté quickly in a very hot pan with minimal olive oil.
Toast until slightly browned, then remove from heat and set aside.
Marinate the Swordfish steaks in lime juice, water, and salt for 1 hour.
Sauce:
======
Grind the toasted oregano, bay leaves, allspice, peppercorns, cumin seeds,
whole clove, salt, cinnamon stick, minced garlic, and sugar together in a grinder or mortar.
Add 3/4 cup of water and 1/2 cup red wine vinegar to a sauté pan. Simmer for 15 minutes.
Assembly:
=========
Remove the Swordfish steaks from the marinade, discard the skins,
and pat them dry. Heat olive oil in a sauté pan and cook the fish until it has a light golden crust.
Place the cooked fish on a serving dish. Deglaze the pan with 1/4 cup red wine vinegar.
Add the herb and vinegar sauce to the pan and bring to a boil. If necessary, add water
to thin the sauce. Spoon the sauce over the fish and garnish with sliced onions and chilies.
Serve at room temperature.
